Home › Programmes › Diploma in Pharmacy
Diploma Programme

Diploma in
Pharmacy

The 3-year 'Diploma in Pharmacy' programme at DIT aims to produce individuals skilled in monitoring the quality, safety and dispensing of medication. Approved by MOHE and MQA, the programme combines classroom activities, practical sessions and clinical placements for a holistic learning experience.

3 Years (Full Time) Intake: May & September
Enrol Now

Accreditation

Course Code: DPM MQA: MQA/FA 5392 MOHE: KPT/JPS(N/727/4/0074) HRDF Claimable International ✓

Why Choose DIT?

  • ✓ Full scholarship & job placements from partner hospitals
  • ✓ MOHE & MQA accredited programmes
  • ✓ 30+ years affiliated with Damai Service Hospital
  • ✓ Practical placements at approved hospitals

Advantages of Studying at DIT

MOHE & MQA Accredited — All programmes approved by Ministry of Higher Education
PTPTN Loans — Financial assistance available for eligible Malaysian students
Hostel Accommodation — Established condominiums with full facilities nearby
Guaranteed Job Placements — Scholarship partnerships with partner hospitals
Qualified Faculty — Registered lecturers and clinical instructors
30+ Years of Excellence — Affiliated with the established Damai Service Hospital

Entry Requirements

Local Students

Pass Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) or equivalent with 3 Credits in: ONE (1) science subject (Biology / Physics / Chemistry / General Science / Applied Science) AND TWO (2) other subjects
OR Pass Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) with minimum Grade B in 3 subjects
OR Pass Sijil Kemahiran Mara (SKM) Level 3 in a related field and pass SPM with minimum 1 credit
OR Certificate related to Health Sciences with minimum CGPA 2.00
OR Pass Sijil Tinggi Persekolahan Malaysia (STPM) with minimum Grade C in 1 subject
OR Pass Sijil Tinggi Agama Malaysia (STAM) with minimum Maqbul
OR Any other qualifications recognised by the Malaysian Government

International Students

Pass GCE O-Level or equivalent with minimum Grade C in 3 subjects
OR Any other qualification recognised by the Malaysian Government

About This Programme

The 3-year 'Diploma in Pharmacy' programme at DIT aims to produce individuals skilled in monitoring the quality, safety and dispensing of medication. Approved by MOHE and MQA, the programme combines classroom activities, practical sessions and clinical placements for a holistic learning experience.

Course Modules

1Semester 1

  • Basic Chemistry I
  • Basic Biology
  • Mathematics
  • Introduction to Pharmacy Services
  • Pharmaceutics I
  • Penghayatan dan Etika Peradaban
  • Pengajian Islam / Moral Studies

2Semester 2

  • Organic Chemistry
  • Basic Physiology
  • Basic Microbiology
  • Pharmaceutical Calculations
  • Advanced Communicative English
  • Pharmaceutics II
  • Community Service

3Semester 3

  • Pharmaceutics III
  • Pharmacology I
  • Pharmacology II
  • Dispensing Techniques
  • Behavioural Science
  • IT & Entrepreneurship

4Semester 4

  • Occupational Safety and Health
  • Pharmacology III, IV & V
  • Technical Skills
  • Pharmaceutical Microbiology

5Semester 5

  • Clinical Practice I – Outpatient
  • Clinical Practice II – Pharmacy Store
  • Clinical Practice III – Galenical Preparations

6Semester 6

  • Clinical Practice IV – Inpatient
  • Clinical Practice V – Clinical Pharmacy Unit
  • Clinical Practice VI – Drug Information Unit

Career Prospects

Pharmacy Assistant (hospitals & clinics)Retail PharmacistSales Representative (pharmaceutical companies)

Future Education Pathways

→Diploma → Degree in Pharmacy
→Degree → Master in Pharmacy / Clinical Pharmacy

Ready to Apply?
